title: amaryllis × rugosa
intro: Amaryllis × rugosa, also known as the Jersey Lily, is a hybrid plant species derived from the Amaryllis belladonna and the Hippeastrum species. The roots and flowers of Amaryllis × rugosa have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, exhibiting antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory problems, and promoting wound healing. Amaryllis × rugosa can be prepared in various forms, including infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be used to treat skin conditions, sore throats, and other ailments.
